rfi_text: The pool barrier design does not comply with clause F9 due to the junctions between the boundary and internal fences. The functional requirement of F9 is to restrict access and prevent unsupervised entry by children under five years of age. In particular, Performance Requirement F9.3.3 requires that barriers surrounding a pool do not have permanent objects or projections on the outside that could assist children in climbing over. In this instance, the boundary fence forms part of the pool barrier. The owner cannot control what happens on the other side of the fence. To prevent a child climbing down from the top of the boundary fence into the pool area, a 900 mm climb-free zone is required. As the current distance between the boundary fence and the pool fence is only 600 mm, there is concern that a child could potentially use the pool fence to gain access to the pool area.
expected_response: Either revise to comply with F9/AS1 paragraph 2.2.1(d) (900mm non-climbable zone with 10mm projection rule), or submit alternative solution evidence of F9.3.1 + F9.3.3 compliance.
source: MBIE Determination 2026/013 §3.3 — verbatim SDC RFI dated 23 December 2025, 46 Bridgehampton Drive, Prebbleton

rfi_text: There is insufficient information about the boundary fence construction. Evidence has not been provided about the spacing of the rails, the presence or absence of projections within 1200mm, and the absence of permanent objects that may assist climbing. Please provide.
expected_response: Detail drawings showing rail spacing, projections within 1200mm, and absence of climb-assist features both internally and on the boundary side.
source: MBIE Determination 2026/013 §3.4 — verbatim SDC RFI 23 Dec 2025

rfi_text: A breach against s40(1) of the Building Act 2004, namely building work in connection with a building (sleepout), where the floor level exceeds 1 metre above the supporting ground. The building work is not provided for under Schedule 1 exemptions. Exemption 3B of Schedule 1 requires the building to be 'not more than 1 storey (being a floor level of up to 1 metre above the supporting ground and a height of up to 3.5 metres above the floor level).' The building, supported at the outer edge by posts that extend well beyond 1 metre above the supporting ground, does not comply. Remedies: (1) Apply for a certificate of acceptance under s96 of the Act, for all work that can be shown to comply with the Building Code, AND (2) Apply for a building consent for any additional building work, OR (3) Remove all the unapproved building work that required building approval.
expected_response: Choose remedy 1+2 (COA + BC), OR remedy 3 (removal). Compliance date: 16 February 2026.
source: MBIE Determination 2026/008 §2.3–2.6 — verbatim CCC Notice to Fix ENF1238496/004 dated 29 October 2025, 22A Glendevere Terrace, Christchurch
